headlines.title: E. Jean Carroll collects more than $5 million from Trump in sexual abuse and defamation case
headlines.description: E. Jean Carroll has collected her $5 million judgment against President Donald Trump, more than three years after a jury found him liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

headlines.title: Supreme Court justices ask Congress for additional security funding
headlines.description: Supreme Court justices are testifying before Congress for the first time in seven years, seeking additional funds to combat a rise in security threats.

headlines.title: Barrett testifies about swatting incident and threatening packages at hearing
headlines.description: During a hearing on the Supreme Court's budget, Justice Amy Coney Barrett shared the security threats she and her family have faced, including a swatting incident and threatening packages sent to her home.

headlines.title: Justice Kagan calls for additional Supreme Court security funding
headlines.description: Supreme Court Justice Elena Kagan delivered her opening statement at a hearing over the court's budget, which includes an increase in security funding as the justices have faced growing threats in recent years.

headlines.title: Trump to Deliver Rare Prime-Time Speech: Here's What to Expect
headlines.description: President Donald Trump announced plans to deliver a rare prime-time speech to the nation Thursday night. The president will reportedly address newly declassified intelligence which the administration claims reveals plans by foreign nations to interfere with the 2020 election. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: How Lindsey Graham’s death quickly changes a busy Senate: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.

headlines.title: South Carolina governor appoints Lindsey Graham’s sister to finish his Senate term
headlines.description: President Trump said earlier he recommended Darline Graham Nordone for the position because it would be “a fabulous tribute to Lindsey.”

headlines.title: Tracking the DOJ's effort to get U.S. voter registration data
headlines.description: The Justice Department is seeking voter registration list data from all 50 states and the District of Columbia over the past year.

headlines.title: Republicans scramble to find a replacement for Sen. Lindsey Graham ahead of the midterm elections
headlines.description: President Donald Trump told NBC News he has someone in mind he’d like to replace the late South Carolina senator but declined to name the person.

headlines.title: Judge says Trump admin tried to ‘manipulate the judicial process’ with its IRS settlement
headlines.description: A federal judge in Florida has found President Donald Trump’s lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service was brought in “bad faith” for an “improper purpose.”

headlines.title: Mitch McConnell says he suffered fall, was unconscious in first statement since hospitalization
headlines.description: McConnell, R-Ky., said he would return to the Senate but didn’t give a date.
